sections.paragraph:
καλάνδαι, ῶν, αἱ (Lat. loanw.: calendae.—Dionys. Hal. 6, 48; 8, 55; 9, 67; 16, 3; Plut., Numa 61 [3, 6], Mar. 412 [12, 3]; ins [indices in SIG, OGI]; pap [Preis., III 90]; Jos., Ant. 14, 228; loanw. in rabb.) the calends, the first day of the month in the Roman calendar πρὸ ἐννέα καλανδῶν Σεπτεμβρίων=on August 24 IRo 10:3. πρὸ ἑπτὰ καλ. Μαρτίων on February 23 MPol 21.

Lat. Lat. = Latin

loanw. loanw. = loanword

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 502
